Bulletin Board · Admin Guide

Jump to a slide

Admin

Welcome, admin.

You can do everything an advisor can do — plus override posts, manage advisor accounts, and configure the site.

This guide covers admin-only powers first, then walks through the full advisor workflow so you have it all in one place.

Admin

What admins can do that advisors can't

  • Edit or delete anyone's posts — useful for cleanup, corrections, or take-downs.
  • Manage advisor accounts — add, edit, remove advisors and reset passwords from the Manage Advisors page.
  • Edit office hours & advisor directory on the About page.
ℹ️ Admin accounts today: admin@ebhcs.org and leah@ebhcs.org.
Admin

The Manage Advisors page

In the Advisor Portal you'll see a Manage Advisors page (admins only). Everything you need to add, edit, reset, or remove an advisor lives here — no Firebase console required.

[ Screenshot: Manage Advisors page with the "Add New Advisor" form and advisor cards ]
Admin

Adding a new advisor

  1. Open Manage Advisors in the Advisor Portal.
  2. Fill in the Add New Advisor form:
    • Username — their email prefix (e.g. jsmith).
    • Display name — what students see on bulletins.
    • Email — their @ebhcs.org address.
    • Toggle Admin on only if they should manage all posts and advisors.
  3. Click + Add Advisor.
  4. Share their starting password: ebhcs2025. They'll be asked to change it on first sign-in.
⚠️ Only @ebhcs.org emails work. Anything else will be rejected at login.
Admin

Editing or removing an advisor

Each advisor card on the Manage Advisors page has three buttons:

  • Edit — update their display name, email, or admin status.
  • Reset password — sends them a reset link (or sets it back to the default).
  • Remove — deletes their account. Their existing posts stay; new ones can't be made.
[ Screenshot: Advisor card with Edit / Remove / Reset password buttons ]
💡 The Admin account card has no Remove button — you can't delete the root admin from the UI.
Admin

Removing or overriding a post

When you're logged in as an admin, every post card shows Edit and Delete — even posts you didn't create.

[ Screenshot: Post card with admin override buttons visible ]
💡 Before deleting, consider editing instead — fewer broken links and less confusion for students who already saw it.
Admin

Office hours & advisor directory

The About page shows office hours and the full advisor directory. These were recently added — they're editable from the admin dashboard.

[ Screenshot: About page on mobile with office hours and directory accordion ]
Admin

Who to escalate to

For anything you can't fix from the Manage Advisors page — site issues, bugs, weird errors, deeper account problems — contact Marlie (mcreed@ebhcs.org).

ℹ️ Marlie is the only one with Firebase / backend access. If something seems off behind the scenes, that's the right escalation path.

Next up: the full advisor workflow, in case you ever need to walk a new advisor through it (or do it yourself).

Advisor workflow starts here
Welcome

The advisor workflow, start to finish.

Everything below is what your advisors see. Helpful to know if you're training someone or filling in.

The two big ideas

Bulletins vs. Resources

📣 Bulletins

The live feed. Timely things: a job opening this month, a workshop next week, a deadline. Newest-first.

🧭 Resources

Always there. Permanent help links by topic — housing, immigration, food, health, jobs.

💡 Rule of thumb: if it has a deadline, it's a bulletin. If it'll still be useful next year, it's a resource.
Step 1

Logging in

  1. Scroll to the bottom of any page (or open the About page) and tap Advisor Portal.
  2. Sign in with an @ebhcs.org email.
  3. First time? Default password is ebhcs2025. You'll be asked to change it right away.
[ Screenshot: Advisor login page ]
Step 2

The dashboard, in one look

  • My posts — everything published, with edit and delete.
  • Analytics — views and clicks over 7, 30, or 90 days.
  • Create — new bulletin, calendar event, or resource.
[ Screenshot: Advisor dashboard overview ]
Posting

Post a bulletin

  1. Click Create → Bulletin.
  2. Add a clear title and a short description.
  3. Optional: attach a PDF (max 10MB) or a link.
  4. Hit Publish. It shows up in the student feed instantly.
[ Screenshot: New bulletin form ]
💡 Short titles win. "Summer internship — apply by June 1" beats "Important opportunity for students."
Posting

How the calendar works

There are two ways things end up on the calendar:

📣 Bulletin with a date

If a bulletin has an event date attached (a workshop, deadline, fair), it automatically shows up on the calendar too. Post it once.

🗓️ Calendar event

For date-only items that aren't a bulletin — Memorial Day, First Day of School, February Break, early dismissal. No long description needed.

💡 If you'd write a paragraph about it, post a bulletin. If it's just a date students should know, make it a calendar event.
[ Screenshot: Calendar view showing both a bulletin event and a date-only event ]
Posting

Add a resource

Use this for evergreen help — things that stay useful all year. Pick a category (Immigration, Housing, Health, etc.), give it a title, and paste the link.

ℹ️ All advisors can see and reorder resources — try to avoid duplicates. Search the category first.
[ Screenshot: New resource form with category dropdown ]
Know what's working

Analytics

Each post shows views and link clicks. Switch the date range (7 / 30 / 90 days) to see trends.

[ Screenshot: Analytics dashboard with date toggle ]
💡 Lots of views but no clicks? The title is great but the content needs work.
Cleanup

Edit or delete posts

  • Advisors can edit or delete their own posts any time.
  • As admin, you can edit or delete anyone's — use it sparingly and let the original author know.
[ Screenshot: Post card with Edit / Delete buttons ]
Install it like an app

Add it to your home screen or desktop

📱 iPhone (Safari)

Share button → Add to Home Screen → Add.

🤖 Android (Chrome)

Menu ⋮ → Install app.

💻 Mac / Windows

Chrome or Edge: address bar Install icon → Install.

✅ Why bother?

Opens faster, lives next to your other apps, and works offline for cached posts.

[ Screenshot: Install prompt on desktop Chrome ]
Done

That's the whole tour.

Bookmark this page — re-run it any time, or share the link with a new advisor.

💡 The student version lives at /onboarding/students.html. Send it to a class when you onboard them.
